title: eriogonum elatum subsp. villosum
intro: Eriogonum elatum subsp. villosum, also known as the Woolly or Fuzzy Mormon Tea, is a subspecies of the Eriogonum elatum plant, which is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the buckwheat family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Eriogonum elatum subsp. villosum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Eriogonum elatum subsp. villosum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically made by steeping the dried leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent.
